name: varlen paged grouped-query attention (PREFILL/EXTEND) -- packed multi-token batch, causal

op.signature: out[t,H_q,D] = softmax_{j<(nk-nq)+p+1}(scale * q[t,h] . k_cache[block_table[s,j//bs], j%bs, h//group]) @ v_cache[same]; packed varlen batch over num_seqs sequences, causal

preconditions: GQA divisibility: H_q % H_kv == 0 (group = H_q // H_kv is an integer). RUNTIME assert in the reference + kernel, NOT a decidable constraint (a modulo BETWEEN two shape symbols cannot live in the constraint mini-language -- the lenient dry-run substitutes unbound symbols as 0 and 0 % 0 raises ZeroDivisionError; a modulo by a CONSTANT like K % 128 == 0 is fine). Same pattern as paged_attention (decode).
PREFILL/EXTEND shape: q is [num_tokens, H_q, D] -- a PACKED batch of num_seqs variable-length sequences. This is the complement of paged_attention (decode, q is [B,H_q,D] with ONE token per request).

Causal EXTEND semantics: for seq s with nq new q-tokens (cu_seqlens_q[s+1]-cu_seqlens_q[s]) and nk total kv positions (cu_seqlens_k[s+1]-cu_seqlens_k[s]), the p-th new token (0-indexed) attends to kv [0, (nk-nq)+p+1). The new tokens are the LAST nq positions of the sequence. nk >= nq is required (RUNTIME assert) -- the new q-tokens must be a suffix of the kv. nk == nq is pure prefill (prefix 0); nk > nq is chunked/extend prefill (prefix = nk-nq already-cached KV).
GQA contiguous head mapping: qo head h attends to kv head h // group (the standard vLLM/flashinfer mapping). MHA is H_kv == H_q; MQA is H_kv == 1.

block_table[s, m] is the physical page index into the [Nb,...] pool for sequence s's m-th logical page; the first ceil(nk / block_size) pages are valid. block_size (page_size) is general (1 for mini-sglang's RadixCache; 16 for vLLM). cu_seqlens_k only its per-seq DIFF matters for the paged layout (the kv is gathered via block_table[s], not a flat ragged tensor).
causal_end = (nk-nq)+p+1 <= nk always, so the causal mask doubles as the valid-kv mask (positions in a partial last page beyond nk are never reached).
scale is the softmax scale (typically head_dim**-0.5); it is a dispatch kwarg, not a tensor shape.

numerics.notes: Flash-style online softmax: per (q-token, qo_head), stream the seq's causal paged KV range in BLOCK_N chunks with a running fp32 max/denominator/value accumulator (the reference materializes the per-token attention for clarity; the device kernel streams with a causal bound). bf16/fp16 logits upcast to fp32 for score/softmax/value, cast back on store. Tolerance matches the repo's attention convention (sparse_mla_attention, paged_attention decode): rtol=1e-2, atol=1e-1 for bf16/fp16. CROSS-BACKEND rtol is 0.05 (looser than single-backend 0.01) for an HONEST reason (not hiding a bug): attention outputs span orders of magnitude (some elements near zero), so the RELATIVE error diff/|e| is ill-conditioned -- a machine-epsilon absolute difference divided by a near-zero output inflates rel to a few percent even when the algorithm is exact. Evidence on the decode twin: fp32 max ABS error 4.8e-7 (= fp32 machine epsilon); bf16 adds ~1 ULP on store. This is the same situation flash-attention/vLLM/flashinfer face (1e-2-1e-1 relative bars). The single-card verify gate uses the COMBINED criterion |a-e| <= atol + rtol*|e| (atol absorbs the near-zero regime) and passes cleanly. Causal masking is index-arithmetic-only (no numerics) so it does not affect tolerance.
